refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall minimize energy consumption per ton moved to meet or exceed the customer-defined energy efficiency target.
refined_fr.rationale: Refining the high-level FR into a measurable and actionable sub-FR focused on minimizing energy per ton moved aligns with the acceptance criteria and enables targeted design and verification.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure total energy used and tons moved over the evaluation period and verify that energy_per_ton ≤ E_target.
sub_frs: Minimize energy consumption during vehicle operation
Optimize task scheduling to reduce idle and unnecessary movements
Monitor and report energy usage and tonnage moved

candidate_dps: Intelligent task scheduling and routing algorithms
Real-time energy monitoring and feedback system
selected_dp.selected_dp: Combination of energy-efficient vehicle powertrain design and real-time energy monitoring and feedback system

potential_sources: Interaction between vehicle powertrain and monitoring system
Data exchange between vehicle and scheduling algorithms
coupling_check.notes: The selected DPs are logically coupled but can be modularized with clear interfaces to minimize complexity.

assumptions_risks: Assumes availability of accurate energy and tonnage sensors
Risk of integration complexity between vehicle systems and monitoring software
Potential performance impact if scheduling algorithms are not optimized
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider further decomposition of task scheduling DP if integration risks increase.

im_boundary.name: Vehicle
im_boundary.scope: Physical components responsible for powertrain and energy consumption

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Vehicle sends energy usage and operational data to monitoring systems

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Vehicle receives optimized task schedules to minimize energy use

module_containment_scores.notes: High containment of powertrain and energy consumption functions; low diffusion risk.
Limited containment; mainly remote control, some overlap in tasking interface.
Minimal relevance to energy efficiency functions.
Broad scope; risk of diffusion if energy functions are not well partitioned.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Assigning energy efficiency DPs to Vehicle module maximizes containment and minimizes diffusion, enabling clear ownership and focused verification.
containment_risks: Potential interface complexity with Teleoperation for tasking commands
Risk of duplicated energy monitoring logic if not centralized
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Review interface definitions with Teleoperation to reduce coupling and ensure single source of energy monitoring.

fr_dp_gate.reason: Refined FRs are well defined and selected DPs logically satisfy the functional requirements with manageable coupling.

diffusion_risks: Possible diffusion of energy monitoring logic across Vehicle and NGHE Solution modules
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Vehicle module shows strong containment for powertrain and energy functions; interfaces with Teleoperation and NGHE Solution are clearly identified.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification can be performed by measuring energy consumption and tonnage moved via telemetry interfaces and vehicle sensors.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of energy efficiency functions to Vehicle module with defined external interfaces.

recommended_revisions: Clarify and standardize telemetry data formats to avoid integration issues.
Consider centralizing energy monitoring logic to avoid duplication.

decision.justification: While the decomposition and assignments are sound, interface definitions and potential diffusion risks require refinement before proceeding to next level.
next_level_sub_frs: Minimize energy consumption during vehicle operation
Optimize task scheduling to reduce idle and unnecessary movements
Monitor and report energy usage and tonnage moved

revise.why: To reduce diffusion risks and clarify interface definitions between Vehicle, Teleoperation, and NGHE Solution modules.

assumptions: Energy and tonnage sensors provide accurate data
Task scheduling algorithms can be integrated with vehicle control
open_risks: Integration complexity between Vehicle and Teleoperation modules
Potential duplication of energy monitoring logic
evidence_needed: Interface control documents between Vehicle and Teleoperation
